Real Data. Real Growth.
The Problem
A Great Dealer.
Invisible on Google.

Everglades Equipment Group was already one of the top John Deere dealers in Florida — but online, their YouTube channel was just sitting there. No SEO strategy, no keyword targeting, and no system to turn video content into Google search traffic. They were leaving millions in equipment sales on the table every single year.

"SEO content is very different from viral content. It requires deep research to rank at the top of Google — and most agencies don't know the difference."

— Bakes Media
Our Approach
YouTube as a
Search Engine Asset.

We identified the exact questions John Deere buyers were typing into Google, built long-form videos around those queries, and engineered thumbnails designed to earn the click. The result: Everglades videos ranking on the first page of Google — pulling in 46% of their traffic from external sources, primarily Google Search.

"When your YouTube video ranks on Google page one, you own that customer before they ever walk into a competitor's showroom."

— Bakes Media

146KViews — One Video
Why This Worked
The SEO Difference

Most agencies chase views. We chased buyers. SEO content targets the exact questions your customers are already asking on Google — and positions your video as the answer. Here's how we did it.

🔍
46.2%
External Traffic

Nearly half of all views came from outside YouTube — primarily Google Search. This means buyers found Everglades before they even opened YouTube.

📈
#1 on Google
Search-First Titles

We titled every video based on the most searched buyer questions in the equipment niche — "Zero Turn vs. Belly Mower," "John Deere vs. Kubota" — terms with real purchase intent.

🎯
275K Views
Shorts SEO Too

We clipped the intro as a Short — it got 275K views and ranked in Google's video box independently. Shorts rank for SEO and compound your long-form reach.

🖼️
5.39% CTR
Thumbnail Engineering

Thumbnails were designed to earn the click in Google's video carousel — a friendly face, clear contrast, and a visual answer to the searcher's question.

⏱️
146K Views
One Video, Compounding

"Zero Turn Mower vs. Riding Lawn Mower" — one video, 146K views, 138.9K more than typical performance. A single well-researched SEO video pays dividends for years.

💰
$9M+
Revenue Attribution

Equipment sales tracked directly back to YouTube-driven traffic. When a $50K John Deere tractor buyer finds you on Google first, the channel paid for itself a thousand times over.

The Proof
The Data Doesn't Lie

Every screenshot below is real — pulled directly from YouTube Studio and Google Search. This is what 1.5 years of SEO-driven content infrastructure looks like.

Ranking on Google — Incognito

Everglades Equipment Group appearing in Google's video carousel for high-intent search queries. 46% of traffic came from external sources — mostly Google Search.

Traffic Sources & Revenue Data

2,845,287 total views with 62.6K watch hours. Traffic breakdown shows Google Search, Browse, and Suggested driving the bulk of buyer-intent views.

Year-Over-Year Growth — Before vs. With Bakes Media

Before Bakes Media (365 days): 1.7M views, $717 revenue. Year 1 with Bakes Media: 2.5M views, $3,958. Year 1.5: 2.8M views, 62.6K watch hours, $5,195. The channel compounded every single year.

How It Unfolded
The 1.5-Year Build
Year 0

Before Bakes Media — Baseline

1.7M views over 365 days with $717 in estimated revenue. Solid subscriber base but no SEO strategy, no keyword-targeted titles, and no system to pull Google traffic.

Year 1

SEO Foundation Built

Keyword research, title strategy, and thumbnail engineering deployed. First videos targeting buyer-intent queries go live. Views climb to 2.5M. Revenue jumps 5x to $3,958. External traffic starts compounding.

18 Months

146K Views on One Video

"Zero Turn Mower vs. Riding Lawn Mower" hits 146K views — 138.9K above typical performance. Ranks in Google's video carousel. The clipped Short generates 275K additional views independently.

Ongoing

$9,000,000+ in Attributed Sales

With 46% of traffic arriving from Google Search, every video in the library continues driving high-intent equipment buyers to Everglades — long after the camera stopped rolling. Total sales attributed to content: over $9 million.
